title: Trigraphs should be avoided
description: Trigraphs should not be usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellbased-on-misraobsoletecertsuspiciousWhy is this an issue?More InfoTrigraphs are denoted by a sequence of 2 question marks followed by a specified third character (e.g. ??- represents a '~' (tilde) character and ??) represents a ']'). They can cause accidental confusion with other uses of two question marks.Noncompliant code examplestatic const char str[] = "(Date should be in the form ??-??-??)"; // Noncompliant.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

compliant:
static const char str[] = "(Date should be in the form ?" "?-?" "?-?" ?)";  // Compliant - adjacent string literals
static const char str2[] = "(Date should be in the form ?-?-?)"; // Compliant - avoid double ?
static const char str3[] = "(Date should be in the form ? ?-? ?-? ?)"; // Compliant - space between ?

char msg[] = "What?" "?!"; // Compliant - split the string to avoid trigraphs

checks.message: Trigraphs should be avoided. Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.
